title: Schistosoma mansoni cercariae transformation (without needle)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1uk4b","text":"Free-living aquatic S. mansoni cercariae transform into the first intramammalian stage, called schistosomula or somules, by burrowing in the host skin. Upon contact, cercariae begin to enter the skin and lose their tails, becoming schistosomula. Somules migrate through the epidermis to the dermis to find a small venule or lymphatic vessel to enter the vasculature.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":20,"length":11},{"style":"italic","offset":87,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dd4sc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3ravo","text":"Transformation of cercariae to schistosomula can be mimicked in the laboratory by centrifuging cercariae to remove tails and then culturing the somules in somule media. This method is particularly useful when the number of cercariae is low (i.e. clonal cercariae from an individual snail).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bf88v","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"13avq","text":"Somules can be cultured for several weeks with regular media changes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"38fqt","text":"Media changes and opening of transformed somules to take place in tissue culture hood using sterile techniques","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"5f5c1","text":"Place 1 sterile 15ml falcon tube on ice per snail to be shed","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ckg6t","text":"Pre-chill benchtop centrifuge to 4°C","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fl11v","text":"Prepare 1x DPBS+2% anti-anti and place in 37°C reciprocating water bath (see recipes in \"MATERIALS\" section)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e7pu4","text":"Prepare somule media and place in 37°C reciprocating water bath (see recipes in \"MATERIALS\" section)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"9ed9o","text":"Cercariae are infectious to humans. Please use proper PPE at all times, including a lab coat, waterproof over-gown, long-cuff gloves AQL \u003c=1.5, and face shield.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fh8n4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"76tui","text":"All disposable materials should be placed in biohazardous waste bins","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"rkcr","text":"Glassware should be immersed in a klorsept solution of at least 50ppm for at least 2 hours, rinsed with diH2O and then autoclaved","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":107,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3fa42","text":"Any liquids should be treated with klorsept solution of at least 50ppm for at least 2 hours","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a4qt3","text":"Liquids treated with klorsept should be diluted further and disposed in the drain","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bsm4m","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a6lr7","text":"Schistosomula in suspension are not a risk to humans UNLESS they are injected directly into the blood stream. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Shed cercariae from snails in a 6-well plate (see protocol " Schistosoma mansoni cercariae shedding"). The snails can be shed for up to 2 hrs by collecting cercariae and replacing with fresh water every 30 min

Using a sterile transfer pipette, dispense cercariae into sterile 15ml falcon tubes on ice IMPORTANT. All the following steps are carried out in the tissue culture biosafety cabinet. Keep a beaker containing 70% ethanol in the cabinet and before discarding any aspirating pipette or serological pipette aspirate ethanol to kill any contaminating cercariae in the pipette.
After collecting cercariae, adjust the volume in each 15ml falcon tube to 15ml with sterile water
Incubate tubes containing cercariae for 00:30:00 On ice
Centrifuge the cercariae 2200 rpm, 4 °C, 30 minutes, Eppendorf 5810R centrifuge
Quickly remove the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 10ml pre-warmed 1x PBS + 2% PSF (see "MATERIALS" section for recipes) by gently inverting the tube (do not use pipet to mix)
Centrifuge the cercariae 1500 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes, Eppendorf 5810R centrifuge
Repeat steps 6 and 7 six more times
Resuspend pellet of somules in ~5ml pre-warmed 37 °C somule media (see "MATERIALS" section for recipes)

Place somules in a 6-well plate and top up each well with pre-warmed 37 °C somule media so each has a total of approximately 4ml media Somules on same day of transformation (Image credit: Dr. Gabriel Rinaldi)

Optional: the following day, remove tails with transfer pipet (the tails will have floated to the top of the water column in the wells) Somules the day after transformation, after removing tails (Image credit: Dr. Gabriel Rinaldi)
